programmes.show_name: Dr Christian Will See You Now
programmes.show_description: Dr Christian Jessen and his team of top in-house specialists are on hand to treat Dale, who is ashamed of his body, and Katie, who has two vaginas.
Spinners and Weavers: The evergreen Sir David Attenborough weaves another tale of wonder as he salutes nature's most intricate structures, from spiders' webs to birds' nests.
Armoured Animals: Sir David Attenborough tells us about some amazing animals. Here the focus is on the rhino and the hedgehog.
Curious Imposters: Sir David Attenborough shines the spotlight on nature's most extraordinary creatures. Looking at cuckoos and the way the death's-head hawkmoth steals honey.
Derwentwater: Paul Rose heads to Derwentwater and Borrowdale, where he meets some of the locals who have battled against flooding and learned to live with the extreme conditions.
Spinners and Weavers: The evergreen Sir David Attenborough weaves another tale of wonder as he salutes nature's most intricate structures, from spiders' webs to birds' nests.
Armoured Animals: Sir David Attenborough tells us about some amazing animals. Here the focus is on the rhino and the hedgehog.
Curious Imposters: Sir David Attenborough shines the spotlight on nature's most extraordinary creatures. Looking at cuckoos and the way the death's-head hawkmoth steals honey.
Coniston: In Coniston, Paul Rose enters a retro cycling event on a penny farthing, joins the mountain bikers of Grizedale Forest and enjoys a spot of rock climbing at Dow Crag.
Spinners and Weavers: The evergreen Sir David Attenborough weaves another tale of wonder as he salutes nature's most intricate structures, from spiders' webs to birds' nests.
Armoured Animals: Sir David Attenborough tells us about some amazing animals. Here the focus is on the rhino and the hedgehog.
Eskdale: In Eskdale, Paul Rose has a close encounter with a vulture, and gets to grips with the locals in a spot of Cumberland wrestling and heads for Scafell Pike.
Shopping from Home.
Motoring news and views with those car-crazed chums. Jeremy tests out the Dodge Viper and Richard tries out some incredible prototypes. Joanna Lumley is their fabulous guest.
There's a face-off between the Aston Martin Vanquish S and Ferrari 575, the Smart ForFour is put through its paces and the aptly named Jimmy Carr hits the test track.
The ever-popular motoring magazine, jam-packed with four-wheeled features. Richard takes the Morgan GT 8 for a spin. Actor Christian Slater is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

Another burst of bifocal comedy with the much-missed duo of Messrs Barker and Corbett. This time, the sketches include Coke Up, Sid and Lil, Bear Facts and a musical finale.
Messrs Barker and Corbett offer up more bifocal comedy with witty wordplay and, of course, a delightfully digressive anecdote from Mr Corbett.
Ronnie Corbett and Ronnie Barker offer up their special blend of spoof news items, meandering anecdotes, and brilliantly inventive sketches.

programmes.show_name: How To Build A Motor Car
programmes.show_description: How to Build a London Cab: With factory access, see how a 21st century electric London Taxi is made in 28 hours.

programmes.show_name: How To Build A Motor Car
programmes.show_description: Mercedes S Class: Factory 56 opens its doors as we follow the ultimate configuration of a Mercedes-Benz S-Class, from the chassis build to the main assembly floor.
Shopping from Home.
Yorkshire's HART paramedics race to help a man who has been stabbed in the back with a screwdriver, and there's a fire in a hotel resulting in a desperate search for the people inside.
A man is left with a metal spike impaled in his face after a traumatic incident on a construction site, sparking a huge rescue operation. Plus, medics help a man who was hit by a car.
It's Not All Beaches and Blue Skies: Anna hits the first milestone in Australia without the support of family: Isabelle's 8th birthday. Jay settles into work, but life at home is not so smooth.
